reviews.review_msg: Matt really made this course harder to take with, his exams questions are so hard (already over the materials) and the rubric is not clear for the project and assignments

BUS340. Very interactive and collaborative style of classroom learning. Workload was light, rubric and grading scheme was clear. Presentation guidelines too strict, basically need to get down to a Tee in terms of timing to not lose marks.

This course goes side by side with 203 and both are waste of my parents hard earned money. The instructor presented statistics suggesting that students who attend class perform very well, but my experience didn't reflect that. The grading criteria were not always clear and I did not understand how evaluations were being made.

This is actually for a BUS340 course. Matt is amazing, teaches well, cares about his students, and classes are always engaging. Left the class getting to know many students and not wanting the class to end.

Matt was not the best professor to help newly transferred students into Beedie. Communication with him was extremely difficult; it was apparent that this course was not his priority. He stated he was teaching 4 courses, a reason for poor communication. However, it negatively affects students who need help when TAs can't answer.
The prof is good. Tough grader.

Worst class I've ever taken in university. The grading was extremely bad and putting hours into an assignment gets you maybe a 70%. Don't even get me started about the in class assignment and written section on the comprehensive exam.
Matt's course is tough, if you are looking for a slack course, you probably shouldn't take it with him. He is a good prof, but if you  put the time in, you will do well.
Matt is a nice dude and a decent prof but the class was just pointless honestly. Grading was pretty harsh but obviously it's curved so as long as you're above average you'll do fine.

I had a frustrating experience in Professor Matt's BUS 201/202 courses. During office hours, I was told no guidance could be given before assignment submission. The task required summarizing 50 pages into one page, which felt unrealistic without support. Overall, I found the courses unhelpful and unclear in purpose.

Overall I would say that Matt is a good professor. To do well in this class you need to ask him for  feedback. Try to go to his office hours and talk to him about what he is looking for, for the written exam. He will tell you exactly what you need to do and if you follow his instructions you will do well. TALK WITH YOUR TA about what they expect.
Matt was a nice prof and my rating is mainly towards the course itself. The course is useless as you don't gain anything from it. It is a waste of money and the best part of this course was nothing.

This is a review for BUS 340 but that wasn't an option to click. Professor Matt was a great lecturer and gave g cool assignments. But, he was super disorganized and it felt impossible to tell which things were due when, and sometimes you couldn't find links for things, or he didn't tell you you needed something until after they were due.

So, firstly, this review is for BUS 340 but that wasn't an option to select. Professor Matt was an awesome guy, super approachable, good lectures, interesting assignments. But, he was super unorganized. Impossible to plan ahead because the expectations were super unclear, and he would submit the rubric after the assignments were due. Timing hellish
Enjoyed the class and group project, it is one of the few classes where I applied what I learned in other courses. He is open to questions and seems to care about how you are doing.

BUS 201 is a pretty useless class as it's pass/fail. Matt was a really great professor though, he talked about his own experiences at SFU as well as his career path, which was inspiring. Just complete the assignments and you'll do fine. Reach out to him outside of class too for some good career advice!

This is a pass/fail class, very easy, he clearly wants everyone to pass, and gives extra opportunities to those who are failing and need a boost. The lectures are great, and he explains his own experiences at SFU and his career path, which is very inspiring. He is not just a great prof, but a great person.

My low rating is directed at the course itself rather than Matt, as he is a nice guy and a great teacher. However, marking across the Ta's is extremely inconsistent and ambiguous regarding the criteria for certain assignments. I don't think this should be a mandatory course for transfer students as many of us know what our concentration will be.

Matt is a caring prof and a nice dude.  He is frequently available outside of class to help with clarification on assignments and projects.  His lectures were interesting, but the class structure was questionable.  Half of the class time was usually spent discussing assigned readings with classmates, which made a number of students disconnect.
BUS 202/201/100 are all the same, all useless, waste of time and money. This is the reason why ppl think business is for kids
Worst professor ever do not take classes with him.

His distribution for assignments are really unbalanced and the grading system is random. Be prepared to untrust any of the "feedbacks" he gave you since it didn't reflect on your real grade at all. The topics being taught are boring, which resulted in the unengaging discussions. He cares a lot about the performative effects than the content itself.

Honestly, he wasn't as bad as the ratings made him out to be and I think its because he looked at the feedback he had gotten. The prof was good but the class was useless with the content and groupwork you had to do. There were weekly in-class discussions which felt pointless and only one midterm where you had to memorize all the readings.
He's a cool guy, very nice. The class itself is fairly easy as it is only 1 credit and very few assignments, lectures are good not too boring and he's very open to chatting with students
Really cool guy. Lectures were boring but that's just how the class is so cant blame him.

This is the most stupid and ridiculous class Ive ever taken and heard of in my life. This class is stupid, useless, nonsense and you can't luterally learn anything from. It's a waste of money, they have this class for taking the money from you!

His grading criteria is very vague, which is made worse by the fact that he takes a very long time to get grades back for assignments. Also, specifically for 202 since the concepts are very basic, his exams are marked in a way that you have to word for word explain the concepts or else you may end up with a lot of part marks or even no marks.

For BUS478, it was essentially a case comp class. One midterm, no final, two presentations, many discussions. Make sure to pick a good group. Hardest part is the midterm - make sure to be thorough and pace yourself. Matt marks tough too. Matt is a nice person but lectures can get a bit dull. Lots of bland discussions throughout the lectures too.

I think Matt Martel is one of the better profs for bus 217 but this course is horribly organized, with unclear rubric with hard marking. A great portion of your grade will come from group work so pray you have a good group. Theres in class writing stuff and it's hell. Brace yourselves for this course.
Class was lazily administered - material and grades delivered late. Assignments were extremely unclear and graded very tough.

He's not bad for BUS 217. One thing to note that this course doesn't really teach anything notable, and you'll mainly have to figure things out from trial and error. There are strict business guiding principles for major assignments so keep that in mind. Averages are low for them though (~55%). Just try your best and hope for the best.

Waiting for his marks was brutal. Marking criteria was very subjective and unclear. He also never released participation mark on canvas. His class and assignments are messy and impossible for improvement. He failed to release quizzes for students to prep for the test as well. 
This course supposed to be grade booster until Matt is your instructor.

His course lacks organization, with lecture slides often posted last minute or not at all. Reading materials for assignments are released just before the deadline and are lengthy. Participation is mandatory, and the grading system is subjective and harsh. He never respond to emails and only provides assignment marks at the end of the semester.
The worst class that I have ever had in my life. Attendance is mandatory; this course is VERY disorganized; he did not mark anything on time, the criteria were very unclear
kinda tougher than I expected for a 200lvl class
Bad
avoid at all cost

Very well spoken and interesting lectures. His curriculum structure is weird though, maybe because he had just started teaching my class. He would often set out assignments that he had not taught how to do yet, which was frustrating. But overall very interesting class and a fun professor.
Matt is a great prof - Mic drop. The final was a little bs, but a positive learning environment. Best class I had this semester.

All BUS201 classes follow the exact same material, and very similar grading structure (the final exam was the exact same thing for each professor), so the professor doesn't matter too much for this class. Martell was a good prof who explained concepts well and kept a fairly laidback lecture. 3-4 hrs of work per week. I don't regret choosing him!

I wish people told me this before taking BUS 201: there isa lot of memorizing we have to gobble up the textbook. thats just how the course is formatted not Matts fault. honestly take kim jang her exam is open book, I heard it supposed to be harder, but lets be fr its business, how hard can it get. other then that matts alright, kinda boring for 3hr
Super cool guy! Funny and chill. Loves Star Wars

Lots of assignments barely made it through with the number of readings and assignments due. Your mark depends heavily on the TA, as Matt does not mark the assignments. I was lucky enough to have great team members for projects. This course was alright but was mandatory.
Matt is such a caring, knowledgable and funny professor. He really cares about students and wants them to succeed. Classes are very interactive and interesting.
Tough, but good. The class is very interactive, if you come prepared, you will do fine.

Matt has by far been my best prof so far. Super caring and wants you to succeed. Go visit him in office hours, He's super helpful and explains things very clearly. Would definitely take a class with him again.

Matt is a really nice and caring professor, and he gives as much material as he can to prepare you, but the pros of taking a class with him stop there. His voice is monotonous and his lectures are him reading off the textbook notes. Tutorials - homework answers read to you. Get ready to read and practice everything yourself.
Good prof. Takes forever to mark stuff though. Lectures are good and if you like beer this is your man

Professor Martell was a great professor for BUS 201! The lectures were engaging and they complemented the textbook material well with real-life examples and applications. I will definitely look for him in my future courses. If you follow the criteria, read through the rubrics, and put in the effort you will get a good grade. Just beat the curve ;)

Matt isn't the greatest professor. His lectures are rather pointless as they are taken directly from the textbooks. Tutorials are useless as well as you just get homework answers read to you. Exams were pretty fair. He is a nice guy but his ability to teach is rather limited.

Prof Martell's lectures are pretty interesting, and he does his best to engage with students. Participation matters! Asking him questions in class generates a great conversation that builds on what is taught. Some funny jokes throughout class, definitely would take again!
The lecture is boring. If you want to get high grade, be sure to read books, go to lectures, do questions he suggested and be active at class. I did all of these, and get an A.

Not the greatest lectures, but the course was premade by the staff, and all classes used the same lectures. Answered emailed and questions usually within in 4 hours, but always expect a reply within 24 hours.  Fun prof, and a great person overall.  Lectures were three hours long, but def my favourite, sometimes boring however.

Very boring lectures. Mid term was easy (if you study) but the avg was pretty low. Final case study was super hard since we only did one practice case study throughout the whole term. HBR articles are boring. Has high expectations for projects and individual assignment so marked very hard (definitely need to put in the effort to get a decent mark).
